message:
Last summer I was tracking a storm moving in, and about 20 minutes before it hit, my phone died. Not low battery—*dead*. I had checked weather updates obsessively all morning, but suddenly I was flying blind, no alerts, no real-time radar, nothing. My partner had to fill me in from their phone, and I felt this weird helpless flutter I wasn't expecting.

Turned out fine—the storm wasn't as bad as predicted—but it made me rethink how much I rely on one device. Now we keep a battery bank charged year-round, and honestly, I've gotten way less anxious about severe weather since I stopped putting all my eggs in the phone basket.

Have you got a backup plan for when your tech inevitably betrays you? A radio, a neighbor's updates, anything?
